<!-- Generated by the plan-only Analysis-Module review workflow (run wf_a0842f94-1c2), 2026-06-07. Read-only review; no application code. -->

# EMOS Analysis Module — UX Review — 2026-06-07

## Summary

The brief proposes a powerful, fully-serializable `AnalysisQuery` engine that drives every chart, pivot, and saved view. That data model is sound. The **UX risk is entirely in the surface area**: a raw rows/cols/measures/filters/scope/state/normalization/viz builder is a pivot-table editor that even expert coaches will not learn cold. The single most important UX decision is to make the product **presets-first**: a coach almost never starts from a blank `AnalysisQuery` — they open a named preset, see a result immediately, then tweak it inline (a "config panel" that mutates the live query) and optionally save the tweak. The blank builder exists but is the power-user escape hatch, not the front door.

The second-largest risk is **planned/performed/delta/adherence overload**. `state` is correctly a first-class axis, but rendering all five states at once in a pivot cell or chart series produces noise. The fix is a single global "compare mode" control (`Performed only · vs Planned · Δ · Adherence`) that reshapes the whole view at once, with planned shown as a muted reference (the existing `PlannedVsPerformed` ghost-bar/dashed-area treatment is the right idiom and should be generalized), never as an equal-weight second series.

Third: **normalization must be visibly active**, not buried in a dropdown. Group comparison with no normalization is actively misleading (a 105 kg lifter dwarfs a 55 kg lifter on tonnage), so the active normalization mode needs a persistent, unmissable label on the axis/header — not just a setting.

On **reuse**: the existing analysis code is ergonomically a mixed bag. The *information design* is good and worth keeping (the `PlannedVsPerformed` planned-vs-performed table with inline compliance bar + tonnage-gap delta; the macro-phase ribbon; the zone-distribution-vs-target table; the Recharts wiring for all needed `viz.type`s except heatmap/radar). The *implementation* is off-brand and must not be copied: every analysis component hand-rolls `bg-blue-600` buttons, `bg-gray-50` cards, `text-gray-*`, and local US-style `formatWeek` date formatters — the exact patterns CLAUDE.md's design-system section forbids. The rebuild must route all of it through the shared primitives (`Button`, `Select`, `StatGrid`/`StatCard`, `DataTable`, `SidePanel`, `Ribbon`, `Badge`, `StandardPage`) and `dateUtils`. None of these primitives are used by the current analysis module today.

`PivotBuilder` is misnamed (it is a single-series chart configurator with enum-hardcoded metrics) and should be replaced, not salvaged. `QuickAnalyses`'s preset-gallery-then-drill shell is the right interaction skeleton and should be promoted to the module's front door.

## Builder interaction model (proposed UX)

**Front door = preset gallery, not a blank builder.** Reuse the `QuickAnalyses` card-grid pattern (but rebuilt on shared primitives): a dense grid of preset cards, each a saved `AnalysisQuery` with title, one-line description, and a tiny inline sparkline/thumbnail of its current result. Clicking a card renders the result full-width immediately — zero configuration to first insight.

**Tweak inline via a left config rail, not a wizard.** Once a preset is open, the result sits center/right; a persistent, collapsible **config rail** (left, ~280px) exposes the live `AnalysisQuery` as labelled compact controls grouped top-to-bottom in reading order: `Scope` → `Subjects + Normalization` → `Rows / Cols` → `Measures` → `Compare (state)` → `Viz`. Every control mutates the query and re-renders live (debounced) — this is the "spreadsheet pivot" mental model, not a multi-step flow, and it honours "avoid wizards/modals, prefer inline." A "Reset to preset" and "Save as…" sit at the rail top.

**Dimension pickers as chip-rows, not nested menus.** `rows` and `cols` are each a horizontal add-chip row: click `+ Add dimension` → a flat popover list of the ~14 `Dimension`s (grouped: Who / What / When), click to add a chip; drag chips to reorder; X to remove. `measures` is a compact stacked list, each measure = `[metric ▾] [agg ▾] [state ▾]` on one line with a `+ Add measure` button. This mirrors how coaches already think ("show me reps and tonnage, by exercise, per week").

**Scope is a single segmented control** (`Date range · Macro · Rolling`) that swaps its own sub-control (two `DateInput`s / a macro picker / a window-days number + anchor). Defaults to the athlete's current macro when one exists.

**Save flow = name + scope-binding choice.** "Save as…" opens a tiny inline form (not a modal): name field + a radio for whether the saved view pins absolute dates or stays relative (rolling/macro). New saved views appear as user presets in the gallery alongside the seeded ones, visually separated ("My views" vs "EMOS presets").

**Blank builder is the escape hatch.** A single "New blank analysis" card in the gallery opens the same config rail against an empty query. Power users live here; everyone else starts from a preset and tweaks. This satisfies "learnable by a coach" without dumbing down the engine.

## Findings

### UX-01 — Builder must be presets-first; a blank rows/cols/measures editor is not learnable cold
- **Phase:** 1 (and 2 for the gallery)
- **Issue:** The raw `AnalysisQuery` (rows[], cols[], measures[].agg/.state, filters[], scope 3-modes, normalization, viz) is a full pivot-editor surface. Even expert OWL coaches reason in concrete reports ("planned vs performed reps by week"), not in abstract pivot grammar. A blank-first builder will be abandoned.
- **Proposed UX:** Gallery → open preset → tweak in a persistent left config rail (live mutation of the query) → "Save as…". Blank builder is one card in the gallery, not the landing state. Promote `QuickAnalyses`'s card-grid + drill shell as the front door; rebuild it on shared primitives.
- **Requires engineering:** Yes
- **Risk:** H
- **Effort:** L

### UX-02 — planned/performed/both/delta/adherence overload one global "Compare" control, not 5 stacked series
- **Phase:** 1 (cells) and 2 (charts)
- **Issue:** `state` as a measure-level axis means a single pivot can request planned + performed + delta + adherence simultaneously, producing 4× columns per metric and unreadable multi-series charts. Five states at equal weight is noise.
- **Proposed UX:** A single global **Compare** segmented control: `Performed · vs Planned · Δ · Adherence %`. It reshapes the entire view at once. In `vs Planned`, planned renders as a muted reference (grey ghost bar / dashed area / faint "/ planned" sub-value in a cell) and performed as the solid accent — generalize the existing `PlannedVsPerformed` idiom (ghost bars `#e5e7eb`, dashed planned-tonnage area). In a pivot cell, default to one primary number with an optional secondary; e.g. `vs Planned` cell shows `412 / 450` (performed / planned, planned dimmed), `Δ` shows `−38` coloured, `Adherence` shows `92%` with a 1.5px inline micro-bar (reuse the table compliance-bar pattern). Per-measure `state` override stays available in the rail for power users but is not the default interaction.
- **Requires engineering:** Yes (engine already supplies all states; this is presentation)
- **Risk:** H
- **Effort:** M

### UX-03 — Cell drill-down uses the shared `SidePanel`, inline, never a modal/wizard
- **Phase:** 1
- **Issue:** The brief requires expanding a cell to underlying sessions/exercises. A modal stack would violate "avoid wizards/modals."
- **Proposed UX:** Clicking a pivot cell (or a chart bar/point) opens the existing `SidePanel` (`width="detail"` 320px, or `editor` 480px for dense facts) docked right, keeping the pivot visible — no context loss. Panel header echoes the cell coordinates ("Snatch · Week 14/04 · Performed"); body is a dense `DataTable` of contributing sessions → exercises → set lines (planned snapshot vs performed, reusing the Stacked Notation read idiom for prescriptions). The pivot itself does inline row-expansion for one level of `rows` drill (chevron disclosure on the row label) so a coach can stay in the grid for hierarchy and only open the panel for the leaf facts. `DataTable.onRowClick` and the existing `SidePanel` make this near-free.
- **Requires engineering:** Yes (wiring; primitives exist)
- **Risk:** M
- **Effort:** M

### UX-04 — Active normalization must be persistently visible on every group comparison, not hidden in a dropdown
- **Phase:** 3
- **Issue:** Comparing athletes on raw tonnage/load without normalization is misleading by bodyweight; the brief flags this. If normalization is a dropdown the coach forgets is set, the chart lies silently. Sinclair has zero existing implementation, so its presence/absence is invisible today.
- **Proposed UX:** When >1 subject is selected, the normalization control surfaces inline next to the subject picker AND its active mode is stamped on the view: an axis-title suffix and a small persistent `Badge` in the result header — e.g. `Tonnage (Sinclair-normalized)` / `Load (per kg BW)` / `(per-athlete mean = 100)`. Default the control to a non-`none` mode whenever multiple subjects are chosen (see decisions). Show an unobtrusive caption when `none` is active with multiple athletes: "Raw values — not bodyweight-adjusted." Use `Badge`, not a new component.
- **Requires engineering:** Yes (Sinclair compute is greenfield per the config map)
- **Risk:** M
- **Effort:** M

### UX-05 — Metric Registry Settings surface: dense `DataTable`, with guardrails on derived-formula editing
- **Phase:** 0 surface / shipped with 1
- **Issue:** Coaches can add/edit/delete derived metrics composed of base metrics. A free-text formula editor lets a coach create cycles, reference unknown metric ids, or divide by a count that can be zero — silently breaking every view that uses the metric.
- **Proposed UX:** A dense `DataTable` in Settings: columns `Label · id · Unit · Kind (base/derived) · Applies to (planned/performed chips) · Formula · default agg · actions`. Base metrics are read-only (greyed, no edit/delete — they are the engine's vocabulary). Derived metrics are add/edit/delete. The formula editor is **not** free text: it is a guided composer — pick metric ids from a dropdown of existing metrics (so no unknown-id references possible), choose an operator (`+ − × ÷`), with a live preview computing the result against the currently-selected athlete/period and a red inline validation line. Guardrails: reject self/circular references (a derived metric can only reference metrics defined before it / detect cycles), warn on division where the denominator can be 0, require unit to be chosen, and block delete of a metric referenced by another derived metric or a saved view (show "used by: …"). A reset-to-default for any seeded derived metric (`snatchCleanRatio`, `pullPctOfTotal`). Reuse `DataTable` + `Select` + `Badge`; this is also where the orphaned `intensity_zones` / `lift_ratio_targets` / `compliance_warning_threshold` config (today schema-present but UI-less, per the config map) finally gets an editor.
- **Requires engineering:** Yes
- **Risk:** M
- **Effort:** M

### UX-06 — Print/export: a dedicated print stylesheet + a single export menu; feasible, reuse `PrintWeek` pattern
- **Phase:** 4
- **Issue:** Recharts is SVG and prints, but the on-screen config rail, gallery chrome, and tab bar must not. A naive `window.print()` prints the whole app shell.
- **Proposed UX:** Reuse the established `PrintWeek` / `PrintWeekDesigner` print-route pattern: a print view that renders only the active query's result (header with title, scope, active normalization badge, subject list, generated-on `DD/MM/YYYY HH:MM`, then the chart and/or pivot table) under a `@media print` stylesheet that hides chrome. One `Export ▾` button in the result header: `Print` (opens print view), `PNG/SVG` (chart only — serialize the Recharts SVG), `CSV` (the long-format result rows — straight from the same query output that fills the pivot, so no separate aggregation). Tables print better than charts at A4; default the print layout to "table + small chart" for density. Feasible with current stack; no new packages needed for Print/CSV/SVG (PNG needs an SVG→canvas step — flag as the only non-trivial bit, or ship SVG-only first).
- **Requires engineering:** Yes
- **Risk:** L
- **Effort:** M

### UX-07 — Reuse map: keep the information design, replace the chrome (off-brand, hand-rolled, US-date)
- **Phase:** all
- **Issue:** Every existing analysis component hand-rolls UI against raw Tailwind: `bg-blue-600` active pills/tabs (`AnalysisPage`, `PivotBuilder`), `bg-gray-50` cards, `text-gray-400/700`, bespoke spinners, and local US-style `formatWeek`/`formatWeekLabel` (`${d.getDate()}/${d.getMonth()+1}` built from a non-UTC `new Date(ws)`). None of the shared primitives (`Button`, `Select`, `StatGrid`, `DataTable`, `SidePanel`, `Ribbon`, `Badge`, `StandardPage`) are used. This is precisely the "vibe-coded patchwork" CLAUDE.md's design-system section was written to stop, plus a DST/locale date bug.
- **Proposed UX (salvage vs replace, ergonomic lens):** KEEP the *layouts/idioms* — (a) the `PlannedVsPerformed` weekly table with inline compliance micro-bar + signed tonnage-gap, the single best planned-vs-performed cell pattern (generalize for UX-02); (b) the macro-phase ribbon (re-skin onto `Ribbon`); (c) the `IntensityZones` "your % vs target Δ" comparison table; (d) the `QuickAnalyses` gallery→drill shell (→ UX-01); (e) the StatGrid-style summary cards (re-skin onto `StatGrid`/`StatCard`). REPLACE wholesale — `PivotBuilder` (not a pivot; enum-hardcoded metrics; no rows/cols; summary math in-component) and `AnalysisPage.getDateRange` (period control → the UTC scope resolver, with a proper `macro`/`rolling` mode). All date formatting → `dateUtils` (`formatDateShort` DD/MM, `formatDateRange`). All buttons/tabs/cards/tables → shared primitives. Keep all data-driven colours (phase, zone, category, compliance traffic-light) untouched per the design-system rule.
- **Requires engineering:** Yes
- **Risk:** M
- **Effort:** L

### UX-08 — Coach vs athlete views: Analysis is coach-facing; do not expose the builder to athletes
- **Phase:** 1/3
- **Issue:** The brief is coach-centric (multi-subject, group comparison, registry editing). Athletes have their own dense v2 screens (`TodayScreen`, PR screens). Surfacing a configurable analysis builder in the athlete app would conflict with the read-only-plan data-integrity rule and overwhelm a single-athlete user.
- **Proposed UX:** Analysis builder + registry live only in the coach app. If any athlete-facing analytic is wanted later, it is a *fixed, read-only* rendering of a specific saved `AnalysisQuery` scoped to that athlete (no rows/cols/measures editing, no other athletes selectable) — never the builder. Flag for the synthesizer: confirm scope is coach-only for v1 so the builder isn't accidentally routed into `/athlete/*`.
- **Requires engineering:** No (scoping/routing decision)
- **Risk:** L
- **Effort:** S

### UX-09 — Density target: one screen, no scroll-to-configure; result and controls visible together
- **Phase:** 1/2
- **Issue:** EMOS is dense Fachsoftware; a builder that stacks controls above a chart (as `PivotBuilder`/`AnalysisPage` do today, pushing the chart below the fold) forces scroll-tweak-scroll loops — high interaction cost.
- **Proposed UX:** Two-pane layout: persistent left config rail (collapsible to icons) + result pane that fills the rest. Controls and output are co-visible so every tweak's effect is seen without scrolling. On narrow widths the rail collapses to a "Configure" toggle. This is the standard dense-analytics layout and matches the expert-tool priority over whitespace.
- **Requires engineering:** Yes
- **Risk:** L
- **Effort:** M

## Open questions for the user

1. Is the Analysis module strictly coach-facing for this rebuild, or is an athlete-facing read-only view in scope (affects routing and UX-08)?
2. For group comparison, what default normalization do you want pre-selected when >1 athlete is chosen — `perAthleteMean` (no extra inputs needed) or `sinclair` (needs sex + bodyweight + Sinclair coefficients, currently not implemented anywhere)?
3. Which 5 presets ship first (the brief suggests planned-vs-performed, lift ratios, intensity-zone distribution, competition-lift trend, weekly stress curve)? This drives gallery content and which existing components are worth re-skinning vs rebuilding.
4. Should saved views be a real owner-scoped table now, or localStorage/in-memory until later? This changes whether the "Save as…" flow is durable across devices in v1 (it is a UX promise either way — a coach expects saved views to persist).
5. For derived-metric formulas, is a guided composer (pick metric ids + operators) sufficient, or do you want a free-text expression field (higher power, far higher break-risk — needs a parser + cycle detection)?

## Note on scope

Per the prompt I evaluated the brief only; I did not propose schema or aggregation internals. The Metric Registry Settings editor (UX-05) and saved-views persistence (UX-04/decision 4) are the only places UX touches data shape, and both are surfaced as gated questions, not applied designs.
